policy_commitment: Portugal commits to achieve carbon neutrality (net-zero greenhouse gas balance) by 2050 as established by the Council of Ministers resolution.
source_quote: It shall be stressed that the Portuguese carbon neutrality goal in 2050 has been established in the Resolution of the Council of Ministers n.º 107/2019, which as above mentioned it also approved the Carbon Neutrality Roadmap 2050 (RNC2050) that constituted the Portuguese Long-Term Strategy submitted to the European Commission and to United Nations Framework Convention for Climate Change (UNFCCC).
has_measurable_target: Yes - carbon neutrality (net zero balance between emissions and removals) by 2050.
has_legal_mandate: Yes - established by Resolution of the Council of Ministers n.º 107/2019 (national political/legal instrument).
extraction_notes: National mitigation target established in a Council of Ministers resolution; explicit, numeric, time-bound objective (carbon neutrality by 2050). This is a national policy commitment (long-term mitigation goal) and is suitable for extraction because it is a stated commitment with a clear quantifiable target and a named binding instrument.
causal_mechanism: Human activity → ↓GHG emissions and/or ↑carbon sinks → ↓atmospheric forcing
policy_instruments: National net-zero target / commitment; Long-Term Strategy / Roadmap for carbon neutrality (policy planning and sectoral decarbonization framework)
classification_signals: "carbon neutrality"; "net-zero greenhouse gas balance"; "Carbon Neutrality Roadmap 2050"; "Long-Term Strategy"; "goal...in 2050"

policy_commitment: Portugal approves the Adaptation to Climate Change Action Programme (P-3AC) to implement adaptation measures through defined lines of action, monitoring indicators and targets for 2020 and 2030, and to integrate these measures in sectoral policies and funding instruments.
source_quote:
To approve the Action Programme on Climate Change Adaptation (P-3AC), annexed to this resolution and which forms an integral part thereof.
The P-3AC aims to achieve the 2nd ENAAC objective - Implement adaptation measures - particularly at the level of physical interventions with direct impact on the territory.
has_measurable_target: Yes - P-3AC sets short-term (2020) and medium-term (2030) targets for multiple indicators and identifies funding envelopes for lines of action (see Annex II and funding tables).
has_deadline: Yes - defines short-term (up to 2020) and medium-term (up to 2030) implementation horizons and requires annual monitoring; resolution takes effect the day following publication.
has_legal_mandate: Yes - approved by the Resolution of the Council of Ministers Nº. 130/2019, of 2 August 2019 (national-level programmatic legal instrument).
has_geographic_scope: Yes - national (Portugal); lines of action include national, regional and local measures.
extraction_notes: P-3AC is an Action Programme for adaptation formally approved by the Council of Ministers (Resolution Nº.130/2019). It names concrete lines of action, assigns responsible entities, defines monitoring and reporting obligations, and contains numeric targets and funding envelopes (see Annexes). This meets the policy definition (stated commitment with specific interventions, targets, and monitoring).
policy_instruments: National adaptation action programme; defined lines of action; monitoring indicators and targets (2020; 2030); mainstreaming/integration into sectoral policies and funding instruments; physical interventions at territorial/land-use level (e.g.; infrastructure or land management measures implied)
classification_signals: "Adaptation to Climate Change Action Programme"; "implement adaptation measures"; "monitoring indicators and targets"; "integrate these measures in sectoral policies and funding instruments"; "physical interventions with direct impact on the territory"; "Implement adaptation measures"

policy_commitment: P-3AC establishes monitoring result indicators and numeric targets to be achieved by 2020 and 2030 across municipalities and sectors (e.g., municipal coverage by adaptation plans, sectoral entities having adaptation/contingency plans, water efficiency metrics).
source_quote:
Table 1. Result indicators and targets defined under the NAP (Adaptation to Climate Change Action Programme - P-3AC).
| Result indicator | Target 2020 | Target 2030 |
| Municipalities covered by adaptation plans (municipal, inter-municipal or regional) | 60% | 100% |
| Energy production, transmission and distribution companies that have adaptation plans or contingency plans for extreme events | 25% | 100% |
| Water use efficiency in agriculture | 65% | 80% |
has_measurable_target: Yes - multiple numeric targets (examples: Municipalities covered by adaptation plans: 60% by 2020, 100% by 2030; Energy companies with adaptation plans: 25% by 2020, 100% by 2030; Water use efficiency in agriculture: 65% by 2020, 80% by 2030).
has_deadline: Yes - targets set for 2020 and 2030 (explicit timelines in the Annex II table).
has_legal_mandate: Yes - these indicators and targets are defined under the NAP / P-3AC, approved by Resolution of the Council of Ministers (N° 130/2019); they form the monitoring framework of the national programme.
has_geographic_scope: Yes - national; indicators address municipal coverage and sectoral entities across territory.
extraction_notes: The P-3AC Annex II presents explicit, numeric result indicators and time-bound targets for 2020 and 2030 (e.g., municipalities covered by adaptation plans, sectoral entities with adaptation plans, water efficiency targets). These are quantifiable, national-level policy targets tied to the Action Programme and its monitoring, and therefore qualify as extractable policy commitments.
causal_mechanism: Climate hazard → ↑ adaptive capacity (and ↓ sensitivity/exposure through preparedness)
policy_instruments: Establishing monitoring indicators and numeric targets; mandating or promoting adoption of adaptation plans at municipal/inter-municipal/regional level; requiring sectoral entities (e.g.; energy companies) to have contingency/adaptation plans; setting water use efficiency targets for agriculture (performance metric).
classification_signals: "Municipalities covered by adaptation plans"; "adaptation plans or contingency plans"; "Energy production; transmission and distribution companies that have adaptation plans or contingency plans for extreme events"; "Water use efficiency in agriculture"; "result indicators and targets"; "2020 / 2030 targets"
co_benefits_identified: Improved water resource conservation; reduced agricultural vulnerability to drought; improved continuity/resilience of energy services; reduced economic losses from extreme events; possible secondary reductions in energy/water-related emissions

policy_commitment: Portugal commits to mobilise between 0.15% and 0.20% of its GNI as ODA allocated to Least Developed Countries by 2030.
source_quote: As an EU Member State, Portugal made the commitment to mobilise 0.15% to 0.20% of its GNI as ODA allocated to Least Developed Countries (LDC) by 2030.
has_measurable_target: Yes - mobilise 0.15% to 0.20% of GNI as ODA allocated to Least Developed Countries by 2030.
has_legal_mandate: Yes - stated as a commitment of Portugal as an EU Member State (political/international commitment); not a domestic legal target but an international ODA pledge.
has_geographic_scope: Yes - international (allocation to Least Developed Countries; geographic focus is identified).
extraction_notes: Portugal's international finance commitment for development assistance to LDCs is a time-bound, numeric pledge (0.15%-0.20% of GNI by 2030). This is an explicit policy/financial commitment and therefore extractable.
policy_instruments: Bilateral/multilateral development finance transfers (ODA); budgetary allocation/commitment; concessional grants/loans; international assistance programming
classification_signals: "mobilise ... of its GNI as ODA"; "allocated to Least Developed Countries"; "by 2030"; "Official Development Assistance"
co_benefits_identified: Poverty reduction; economic development; capacity building; institutional strengthening; potential climate adaptation or mitigation depending on fund use

policy_commitment: Portugal established the Portuguese Environmental Fund (2017) to support national environmental policies and international cooperation on climate change; of the climate-related bilateral support cited, USD 5.3 million is considered new and additional to existing ODA flows.
source_quote: The Portuguese Environmental Fund was established in 2017. The purpose of this instrument is to support national environmental policies for the pursuit of Sustainable Development Objectives, contributing to the achievement of national and international objectives and commitments, in particular those related to climate change, water resources, waste and nature conservation and biodiversity. ... Regarding climate change adaptation bilateral cooperation of a total amount of 6.6 million USD, 5.3 million USD is new and additional.
has_measurable_target: Yes - the document states that for climate change adaptation bilateral cooperation of a total of 6.6 million USD, 5.3 million USD is new and additional (explicit amounts).
has_deadline: No specific deadline attached to the allocation statement in the quoted text (fund established in 2017; figures presented for recent years).
has_legal_mandate: Yes - the Environmental Fund was established by decree/legal instrument (document references its establishment in 2017), and it is identified as a financing instrument for environmental/climate objectives.
has_geographic_scope: Yes - national fund with international cooperation focus (support to developing countries, with geographic prioritisation: PALOP and Timor-Leste).
extraction_notes: Creation of a national financing instrument (Portuguese Environmental Fund) with an explicit mandate to support environmental policies and international cooperation on climate change; document quantifies amounts classified as new and additional for bilateral climate adaptation cooperation. This is a resource-allocation policy (legal establishment of a fund and declared use).
policy_instruments: Environmental fund / grant financing for national environmental policies and bilateral adaptation cooperation; provision of development/ODA financing targeted to adaptation and environmental projects
classification_signals: "climate change adaptation"; "climate change"; "support national environmental policies"; "bilateral cooperation"; "nature conservation"; "biodiversity"; "water resources"; "new and additional"
co_benefits_identified: Biodiversity conservation; water resources management; waste management; sustainable development / international cooperation
